app.title: NuxtEdge
app.description: A production-ready Nuxt template with SQLite database, blob storage, KV store, and caching, all running at the edge.

seo.title: NuxtEdge - Build on the Edge
seo.description: A production-ready Nuxt app with database, blob storage, KV store, and caching. Deploy globally in seconds.

hero.description: A production-ready Nuxt app with database, blob storage, KV store, and caching. Deploy globally in seconds.

section.headline: Primitives
section.title: Everything you need, nothing you don't

db.title: Database
db.description: Full SQLite at the edge with Drizzle ORM. Zero cold starts, full CRUD, automatic migrations.

blob.title: Blob Storage
blob.description: Upload, serve, and manage files with automatic CDN caching across 300+ edge locations.

kv.title: KV Store
kv.description: Lightning-fast key-value storage globally replicated, perfect for config, flags, and redirects.

cache.title: Cache
cache.description: Built-in Nitro caching with TTL, stale-while-revalidate, and per-route invalidation. Zero config.

i18n.title: Internationalization
i18n.description: Configure locales in Nuxt and store translation strings in locale JSON files for each language.

aiSdk.title: AI SDK
aiSdk.description: Reusable Workers AI helpers with clean server utilities, model swaps, and streaming-ready endpoints.

email.title: Email
email.description: Send transactional email from the edge with a provider-ready foundation for notifications and workflows.

agents.title: AI DX
agents.description: Preinstalled Agent Skills make it easier to build pages, storage features, and repo improvements in this app.

auth.title: Authentication
auth.description: Session, login, and route-protection primitives are planned next.

websockets.title: WebSockets
websockets.description: Real-time publish and subscribe primitives are planned next.

seo.title: SEO
seo.description: Metadata, social tags, and search-friendly defaults are planned next.

cta.title: Deploy to the edge in seconds
cta.description: NuxtEdge connects your Nuxt app to Cloudflare's global network. One command to deploy database migrations, blob storage, and your full application.

seo.title: AI DX · NuxtEdge
seo.description: Use the repo's built-in Agent Skills with practical prompts and examples.

hero.title: Use AI Agents
hero.lead: Ask for work in plain language. Add `/nuxt`, `/nuxt-ui`, or `/nuxthub` when you want to guide the implementation.

repo.title: Available skills
repo.subtitle: These three cover most tasks in this repo.

installedSkills.workflowHelper: Default workflow helper for general tasks.
installedSkills.nuxt: Best for pages, layouts, routes, composables, and Nuxt-specific work.
installedSkills.nuxthub: Best for DB, KV, blob, cache, and other edge-backed features.

buildPage.title: Build a page
buildPage.prompt: /nuxt create a new `/contact` page and wire it into the navbar.

polishUi.title: Polish a UI
polishUi.prompt: /nuxt-ui improve this settings screen and make the layout feel more intentional.

addStorage.title: Add storage
addStorage.prompt: /nuxthub add a favorites feature stored in the database with a matching API route and UI.

improveDemo.title: Improve a demo
improveDemo.prompt: /nuxt /nuxthub improve the blob demo UI and keep upload/delete fully working.

goodPrompts.title: How to get better results
goodPrompts.subtitle: A few habits improve results.
goodPrompts.knowArea: Use `/nuxt`, `/nuxt-ui`, or `/nuxthub` when you know the area.
goodPrompts.mentionRoute: Mention the route, file, or feature you want changed.
goodPrompts.describeOutcome: Describe the outcome you want, not just the tool.
goodPrompts.verifyChange: Ask Codex to verify when the change should be typechecked or tested.
goodPrompts.goodExample: Good: `/nuxthub` add a simple favorites API and wire it into the UI.

seo.title: AI SDK · NuxtEdge
seo.description: Reusable Cloudflare Workers AI utilities with live generation and streaming demos.

aiSdk.title: AI SDK

lead.afterCode: to connect Cloudflare Workers AI, then reuse one server helper for generate and stream.

aiSdk.usageNote: Local Workers AI development uses Cloudflare's remote service. Authenticate Wrangler first.

demo.title: Live AI SDK Demo
demo.subtitle: Test a Workers AI model through reusable Nitro routes

form.promptPlaceholder: Draft a short release note for a new AI SDK feature.
form.systemPlaceholder: You are a concise staff engineer writing for developers.
form.modelHint: Change the model string to test another Workers AI model without touching the server utility.

output.title: Response
output.idle: Choose Generate or Stream to test the Workers AI endpoints.

errors.fallback: Unable to reach the AI endpoint.
errors.emptyStream: The stream started without a response body.

binding.title: Direct binding
binding.text: Reads `env.AI` from Nitro's Cloudflare event context, close to Cloudflare's official pattern.

utils.title: Reusable server utils
utils.text: One utility normalizes input, validates config, and powers both JSON and streaming endpoints.

seo.title: Internationalization · NuxtEdge
seo.description: Configure languages in Nuxt and organize locale files.

lead.after: then keep the translation strings in

configureLocales.title: 1. Configure locales
configureLocales.body: Add a locale entry here. This defines the locale code, label, and JSON file.

addTranslationFile.title: 2. Add the translation file
addTranslationFile.body: Each file is a JSON dictionary. Keep keys the same across locales and translate only the values.

config.title: Config
config.body: Register each locale in `nuxt.config.ts` with its code and file.

configureTransporter.title: 1. Configure the transporter
configureTransporter.body: Replace the example `nodemailer` values in `nuxt.config.ts` with your provider credentials. Env vars can override only keys already present in config, so keep the full shape.

sendMailParams.title: 2. Set the `sendMail` params
sendMailParams.body: Use `useNodeMailer()` in a server route, server action, or other server-only context. `sendMail()` inherits `from` from Nuxt config, so most calls only need recipient and content.

envOverrides.title: 3. Override secrets with env vars
envOverrides.body: Use env vars for secrets and production-specific values. Prefix overrides with `NUXT_NODEMAILER_` and uppercase the path. Nested keys flatten with underscores.

recommendation.title: 4. Provider recommendation

body.after: for transactional email. It has a clean setup, good deliverability, and simple API key management. If you want plain SMTP portability, Postmark, Mailgun, or Mailtrap also fit well.

baseline.label: Recommended baseline:
baseline.secretPrefix: and put the real secret in

recommendation.footer: Docs first. A later upgrade can add a setup validator or test-send endpoint.

configFields.host: Your SMTP host from the provider.
configFields.port: Usually 587 for STARTTLS or 465 for implicit TLS.
configFields.authUser: SMTP username or API key, depending on provider.
configFields.authPass: Secret credential. Prefer env overrides in deployment.

sendMailParams.subject: Required subject line.
sendMailParams.text: Recommended for clients that do not render HTML.
sendMailParams.from: Usually omit this because the module inherits it from config.

seo.title: Blob Storage · NuxtEdge
seo.description: Upload and serve files through NuxtHub Blob with CDN caching and global delivery.

lead.beforeCode: Upload and serve files with CDN caching using
lead.afterCode: for Cloudflare R2 storage and global edge delivery.

upload.label: Drop an image here
upload.description: PNG, JPG, or WebP up to 1GB. Click or drag and drop.

empty.title: No images yet
empty.description: Upload your first image to store it in Cloudflare R2.

infoCards.title: Edge CDN
infoCards.text: Served from the nearest Cloudflare PoP across 300+ locations.

infoCards.title: Secure by default
infoCards.text: CSP headers apply automatically. No direct R2 bucket access needed.

infoCards.title: No size limits
infoCards.text: Store anything from small config files to large video assets.

seo.title: Cache · NuxtEdge
seo.description: See Nitro response caching with TTL, cache hits, and request history.

lead.beforeBadge: This endpoint is cached with Nitro for
lead.afterBadge: . Watch the value stay the same, then change after the TTL expires.

demo.title: Live Cache Demo
demo.idle: Click to make the first request

note.afterCode: stays the same for 5s. After the TTL, a new random value is computed.

concepts.title: cachedEventHandler
concepts.desc: Wraps a Nitro handler with response caching. Supports TTL, cache keys, and groups.

concepts.title: Stale-while-revalidate
concepts.desc: Serve stale data instantly while refreshing in the background.

concepts.title: Cache invalidation
concepts.desc: Invalidate specific routes or entire feature groups on demand.

seo.title: Database · NuxtEdge
seo.description: Manage edge SQLite data with a simple CRUD demo and schema view.

db.title: Database
db.lead: Full CRUD on edge SQLite, with the same pattern working for other SQL databases.

migrations.title: Migration note

d1.beforeCode: Cloudflare D1 migrations cannot run during build because CI has no database connection. Run
d1.afterCode: locally, or add a CI step before deployment.

migrations.external: For external databases like Postgres or MySQL, migrations run automatically when a database connection is available in dev and build.

composer.placeholder: Write a message to store in SQLite...

empty.title: No messages yet
empty.description: Send your first message above to store it in SQLite.

seo.title: KV Store · NuxtEdge
seo.description: Store redirect rules in NuxtHub KV and apply them through Nitro middleware.

lead.beforeCode: KV works well for small, fast reads like redirects, feature flags, and app config. This demo stores redirect rules in
lead.afterCode: so Nitro can apply them on each request.

editor.title: Redirect Rules
editor.badge: Stored in KV · Applied by Nitro
editor.empty: No redirect rules. Add one below.
editor.footerNote: Changes apply on the next request.

steps.title: Store in KV
steps.desc: Save redirect rules as a JSON object with hubKV().set().

steps.title: Nitro middleware
steps.desc: Middleware reads KV on every request and matches paths.

steps.title: Edge redirect
steps.desc: Matching requests redirect with sendRedirect() without hitting the origin.
